summary:
• U.S.-Iran geopolitical tensions are creating supply volatility and market uncertainty, with escalating sanctions enforcement on Iranian tankers and conflicting diplomatic signals threatening Gulf production stability.

• Canadian oil sands growth is stalling due to policy uncertainty and regulatory challenges, limiting North American supply growth at a time when U.S. drillers cannot meet global demand needs.

• Russian refinery attacks by Ukraine and potential disruptions to Central Asian energy investment from regional geopolitical conflicts pose downstream supply risks to global crude markets.
